title: S1 Ep 1
notes:
In js everything happens inside execution context
In javascript execution context is created intially

 |---------------------------|
 |  Memory    |    Code    --|->code executes one line at a time
 | (Variable  | (Thred of    |              
 | enviroment)| execution)   |
 |------------|--------------|
 |  key:value |              |
 |            |              |
 |  a:10      |              |
 |            |              |
 |  fn:{...}  |              |

 javascript is synchronous single threaded language

title: S1 Ep 2
S1 Ep 3
notes:
Hoisting in javascipt is phenomenon in which we get the value even before we have initialize it

undefined v/s not defined
undefined: A variable that has been declared but has not been assigned a value is undefined
not defined: A variable that has not been declared at all is not defined

Three ways to declare a function:
1. arrow function: var x = ()=>{}
2. function a(){}
3. var a = function (){}

Arrow function behaves like a variable(in memory allocation phase of execution context the arrow function will be allocated a undefined)

For third type of function it also behave like a variable means it will be stored as a undefined

For 2nd type of function only then it is saved as whole function code in memory during execution context phase

Variable declarations are scanned and are made undefined
Function declarations are scanned and are made available

title: S1 Ep 4
S1 Ep 5
S1 Ep 6
notes:
Javascript is loosely typed language/weekly typed language

undefined v/s not defined

undefined:
 It is a special keyword it has its own memory, it is assigned to a variable until the any explicit value has been assigned to it
 Memory has been allocated to the variable
 A variable has been declared but not assigned a meaningful value yet.


not defined:
 Memory has not been allocated to the variable
 A variable that has not been declared at all is not defined

title: S1 Ep 7
notes:
Scope: Were we can access a variable or a function in a code
scope of variable/function: where can i access it
scope is directly dependent on lexical enviornment
Lexical Enviornment: Whenever a execution context is created a lexical enviornment is also created. Lexical Enviornment is local memory along with lexical enviornment of its parent
Lexical: In hierarchy/ in sequence
The way of finding is known as scope chain: Chain of all lexical enviornment and its parent refrence
In memory phase of execution context there is also refrence to its lexical parent
In global level lexical enviornment points to null because it has no parent

title: S1 Ep 8
S1 Ep 9
notes:
Code inside curly bracket is called block.
Multiple statements are grouped inside a block so it can be written where JS expects single statements like in if, else, loop, function etc.
Block values are stored inside separate memory than global. They are stored in block. (the reason let and const are called block scope)
Shadowing of variables using var, let and const.
The shadow should not cross the scope of original otherwise it will give error.
shadowing let with var is illegal shadowing and gives error.
var value is stored in nearest outer function or global scope and hence can be accessed outside block as well whereas same is not the case with let and const.

title: S1 Ep 10
S1 Ep 11
S1 Ep 12
S1 Ep 13
S1 Ep 14
notes:
Callback function => Callback function give us whole async experince in single threaded, synchronous language

Blocking the Main Thread => Main thread in jaavscript is call stack. If some function blocks the call stack then it is called blocking the main thread. Block main thread one function takes a lot of time and due to this other functions are not being invoked

Event listners are heavy as it takes lot of memory so it is good practice to remove them when they are not in work

Garbage Collector and remove event listners =>

title: S1 Ep 15
notes:
Call stack does not have a timer that means if some part of code comes in call stack it do not waits it executes 

Browser has javascipt engine and that engine has call stack

Browser has a lot of features like local storage, timer, geolocation. Now to access these features of browser we need some web api's like fetch(), DOM APIs, setTimeout(), localStorage, console, location

fetch(), DOM APIs, setTimeout(), localStorage, console, location => These all are not part of javascipt these are part of browser. Browser gives us access to use this in js engine using fetch(), DOM APIs, setTimeout(), localStorage, console, location

And these all functions are available in global object that is window

All callback functions gets registers in web api enviornment

When we use setTimeout function we gets access to browser timer and timer starts and callback function gets registered
As soon as timer expires the callback function cannot go to call stack directly so it goes to call stack through the callback queue
After timer expires callback function goes to callback queue and then there is new term that is "Event Loop" 
Event Loop checks callback queue and puts the function in call stack. It checks callback queue for any callback function and puts it in call stack. New execution context is created for the callback function

AddEventListner is given by the browser to the javascipt engine through the window object in form of web api which is DOM api
In AddEventListner the callback functions gets registered and an event is attached to it
ex. If event is click. When user clicks on button the the callback functions is pushed to callback queue and it waits over there for its turn 

Event Loop: It only monitors call stack and callback queue. If call stack is empty and there is a function in callback queue it pushes that function in call stack and it gets executes and also the function gets removed from callback queue
event loop is important because it pushes every callback function one by one to call stack

The callback functions gets registered in web api enviornment and it is present in enviornment until callback is called or the window or browser is closed

There is another queue like callback queue that is Microtask Queue
Microtask Queue has much more priority than callback queue that means function in microtask queue will be pushed to call stack first than callback queue

fetch() => the callback functions from fetch after making network call goes to microtask queue

suppose we have 1000's line of code and in between we have fetch() and setTimeout() functions also and the time of setTimeout() function is over and it is in callback queue and fetch() has also completed it network call and is also waiting in microtask queue but these callback functions in different queue will only be pushed to call stack once the all 1000's line of code is finished that means once the global execution context is poped out of call stack. And event loop monitors the call stack and both of these queue and it pushes the function in call stack. And after call stack is empty then event loop pushes the function of microtask queue in call stack because they have much more priority than callback queue and this is all done by browser

What can come in microtask queue? => all the callback function that come from promises are pushed in microtask queue
What can come in callback queue? => all the callback function that come from setTimeout and event listners are pushed in microtask queue

Callback queue is also called task queue

if there are 3 functions in microtask queue and 1 in callback queue then also event loops pushes 3 functions from microtask queue to call stack after that callback queue function are pushed

Starvation of function in callback queue => when task in call queue do not gets a chance as there are lot of task in microtask queue

Mutation Observer =>

title: S1 Ep 16
notes:
To run javascipt we need => JAVASCRIPT RUNTIME ENVIRONMENT and it consists of :
JS Engine, API, microtask queue, callback queue, etc
But JS engine is most important
These api are different for every environment like browser has different, node has different or some api may be common
some common api are => setTimeout(), console() they both are present in runtime environment of browser and node

1. JS runtime environment contains all elements required to run JS.
2. It contains JS engine, set of API's, callback queue, microtask queue, event loop.
3. JS engine is a piece of code.
4. Process includes Parsing ---> Compilation -----> Execution.
5. Parsing breaks code into tokens and converts it into AST(Abstract Syntax Tree).
6. Modern JS engine follows Just In Time compilation, it interprets while it optimises code as much as it can.
7. Execution and Compilation are done together.
8. Execution has Garbage collector and other optimisation such as inlining, copy elusion, inline caching etc.

Garbage collector => It collects all the unsused function/variable and sweeps it. It uses mark and sweep algortihm

title: S1 Ep 17
S2 Ep 1
notes:
Callback hell
When a function is passed as an argument to another function, it becomes a callback function. This process continues and there are many callbacks inside another's Callback function.
This grows the code horizontally instead of vertically. That mechanism is known as callback hell. and this is known as pyramid of DOOM 

Inversion of Control
The callback function is passed to another callback, this way we lose the control of our code. We don't know what is happening behind the scene and the program becomes very difficult to maintain this process is called inversion of control.

title: S2 Ep 2
S2 Ep 4
notes:
Promise API's
Promise api's returns the promise

settled can be a failure as well as success
Once promise is settled
It can either be resolve/success/fulfilled, reject/failure/rejected => see image

1. Promise.all()
    => It takes array of promises
    => It takes array of promise and resolves it parallely and return an array of all 3 promise
    => It waits for all of them to finish, and collects result and return solution
    => The highest time taken by a any single promise will be the time for promise.all() to return the solution
    => If any one promise fails then promise.all() will be failure and the error message will be thr error message of the promise that have failed
    => If failed promise takes less time than other promises then promise.all() throws error as soon any promise gets rejected. It do not waits for other promises
    => If two promises fails then only that promise error will be displayed which promise failed first
    => It is also known as "Fail Fast"

2. Promise.allSettled()
    => It takes array of promises
    => It do not throws error if any promise fails.
    => It returns the array of objects of result with detail of promise result like fulfilled, reject
    => It waits until the all promises gets settled

3. Promise.race()
    => It takes array of promises
    => It gives the value of first settled promise
    => If the first settled promise gets failed then the error will be thrown of the first settled promise
    => It gives the first result irrespective if it is resolved or rejected

4. Promise.any()
    => It takes array of promises
    => Wait for first settled success
    => The first promise to get resolved is returned
    => Wait for first success
    => It ignores the promise that have been failed before the first resolved promise
    => If all promises fails then it throws error "AggregateError: All promises were rejected"
    => AggregateError will be array of all the failed promise
    => It waits for first success
    => Seaking for first success
    => If all fails then it throws "AggregateError"

title: S2 Ep 5
S2 Ep 6
notes:
Global object is different in browser different in nodejs and different in any other javascript enviornment

In case of browser => "this" keyword in global space always points towards window object
in node it is global not window

"this" keyword works differently in strict mode and in non-strict mode

If "this" keyword is in function then value of "this" keyword depends on strict or non-strict mode

Value of "this" keyword in function is undefined but because the function is in non-strict mode then it will be replaced with globalObject

"this" substitution => If the value of "this" keyword is undefined or null then "this" keyword will be replaced with globalObject only in strict mode

value of "this" keyword depends on how function is called

in strict mode call the function like this window.function() then value of "this" keyword will not be undefined it will be globalObject

If the function is called without any refrence to any object then it is undefined in strict mode.

But in non strict mode the value becomes globalObject

Function vs Method => function as a part of object is called a method

Value of "this" keyword in method is a object

call, apply, bind methods => for sharing method

arrow function dont have their "this" binding
so the arrow function will point at the "this" of its enclosing lexical context

"this" inside DOM elements is refrence to HTML elements

"this" keyword behaves differently in class, constructor
